market_summary: The AI sector is rallying broadly on May 29 as Snowflake's blowout Q1 results — declaring enterprise AI demand at a 'clear inflection point' — lifted software and cloud names across the board; the S&P 500 and Nasdaq were up ~0.5% and ~0.6% respectively on the prior session, with semiconductor stocks also rising today on continued AI infrastructure optimism.

MSFT.headline: MSFT surged ~3.5% as Snowflake's AI inflection-point declaration validated Microsoft's $190B AI infrastructure commitment, with additional tailwinds from a $1B EY partnership and a new coding model announcement.
MSFT.reasoning: Today's rally directly addresses the core bear thesis that has weighed on MSFT all year — whether its massive AI capex is chasing real enterprise demand. Snowflake's results and Morgan Stanley's bullish cloud infrastructure note together confirm the demand signal is real. Shares remain ~12% below their YTD starting point, meaning the long-term holder is still accumulating at a discount to recent levels, with Q3 revenue already beating at $82.9B (+15% YoY) and the next earnings catalyst on July 28.

AMZN.headline: Anthropic raised $65B at a $965B valuation, with Amazon's ~$13B+ stake potentially now worth over $60B on paper, while AWS hit its fastest revenue growth in 15 quarters (+28% YoY) and Truist raised its price target to $320.
AMZN.reasoning: The Anthropic mega-round is a significant paper-wealth event for Amazon, reinforcing its position as the leading cloud partner for the most valuable private AI company in the world. AWS's fastest growth in 15 quarters alongside Snowflake's 5-year, $6B AWS commitment signal durable cloud demand acceleration. Today's mild pullback from recent highs (~$278 52-week high) is noise; the long-term thesis around AWS + AI monetization remains firmly intact.

NVDA.headline: NVDA is up modestly today alongside a broader semiconductor rally, trading ~9% below its all-time high of $236.54 set May 14, following Q1 FY2027 results showing 85% revenue growth to $81.6B and Q2 guidance of $91B (+95% YoY).
NVDA.reasoning: NVDA's near-term price action looks soft relative to the fundamental delivery — 85% YoY growth and guidance of 95% growth is extraordinary for a $5T company, yet the stock remains well off its recent ATH. This divergence reflects investor digestion and some rotation, not a fundamental deterioration. Nvidia's own forecast of $1T+ in data center spending in 2027 rising to $3-4T by 2030 makes the long-term compounding case as strong as ever; next earnings August 26.

GOOGL.headline: Alphabet benefits doubly from Anthropic's $65B raise — its stake (up to $40B committed) could be worth over $100B — while Google Cloud grew 63% YoY to $20B in Q1 and Waymo expanded public robotaxi service to Ojai.
GOOGL.reasoning: GOOGL is a quiet winner today: the Anthropic valuation surge marks a massive paper gain on what was already one of the best AI investments in the market, and it complements Google Cloud's own explosive 63% growth. With Q1 sales up 22% to $110B, operating margins expanding 220bps, and a $4.71T market cap, Alphabet's multi-vector AI monetization (Search, Cloud, Waymo, Anthropic) is compounding well. No action warranted; next earnings July 22-23.

long_term_view: All four portfolio holdings are firing on their core AI and cloud growth theses simultaneously — today's Snowflake-driven software relief rally, Anthropic's mega-raise, and continued hyperscaler capex commitments collectively reaffirm that the AI infrastructure supercycle underpinning MSFT, AMZN, NVDA, and GOOGL is accelerating, not stalling. No long-term concerns today; this is a portfolio built for exactly this environment.
